entries.name: Jenolan Caves
entries.desc: Among the oldest known open cave systems in the world, set in the Blue Mountains west of Sydney. A network of guided show caves reveals limestone chambers, underground rivers, and intricate formations.

photo.alt: Limestone chamber with draperies and stalagmites inside Jenolan Caves
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jenolan-caves.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Jenolan caves in nsw image.JPG" by Cfitzart · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jenolan_caves_in_nsw_image.JPG -->

entries.name: Eisriesenwelt
entries.desc: The largest ice cave in the world, set high in the Tennengebirge mountains near Salzburg. Tours pass towering ice formations lit by carbide lamps, open only during the warmer months.

photo.alt: Frozen ice formations inside the Eisriesenwelt ice cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: eisriesenwelt-ice-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Eisriesenwelt-7.JPG" by Normand Roy · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Eisriesenwelt-7.JPG -->

entries.name: Harrison's Cave
entries.desc: An active limestone cave in the central uplands of Barbados, explored on an electric tram. Flowing streams, pools, and waterfalls run among its stalactites and stalagmites.

photo.alt: Flowstone columns and pools inside Harrison's Cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: harrisons-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Harrisons Cave (6835262484).jpg" by Berit · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Harrisons_Cave_(6835262484).jpg -->

entries.name: Actun Tunichil Muknal
entries.desc: A Maya ceremonial cave in western Belize entered by swimming across a pool. Its inner chambers hold ancient pottery and skeletal remains, including calcified human bones from ritual sacrifices.

photo.alt: Ancient Maya pottery vessels inside Actun Tunichil Muknal cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: actun-tunichil-muknal-pottery.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Actun tunichil muknal-pottery.jpg" by Jkolecki · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Actun_tunichil_muknal-pottery.jpg -->

entries.name: Marble Caves (Cuevas de Mármol)
entries.desc: Marble formations on General Carrera Lake in Patagonia, sculpted over thousands of years by wave action. Their swirling walls reflect the lake's turquoise water and are reached only by boat or kayak.

photo.alt: Turquoise water beside swirling marble cave walls on General Carrera Lake
photo.credit: <!-- photo: marble-caves-general-carrera.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Cuevas de Mármol - Marble Caves, Blue Water, Patagonia, Chile.jpg" by Wescottm · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cuevas_de_M%C3%A1rmol_-_Marble_Caves,_Blue_Water,_Patagonia,_Chile.jpg -->

entries.name: Reed Flute Cave
entries.desc: A limestone cave near Guilin filled with stalactites, stone pillars, and rock formations. Multicolored artificial lighting illuminates the chambers, and ink inscriptions on the walls date back centuries.

photo.alt: Illuminated underground lake inside Reed Flute Cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: reed-flute-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Guilin Reed Flute Cave.jpg" by Romain Pontida · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Guilin_Reed_Flute_Cave.jpg -->

entries.name: Longmen Grottoes
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed complex of rock-cut caves and niches carved into limestone cliffs along the Yi River near Luoyang. Over 100,000 Buddhist carvings — from tiny devotional figures to a 17-meter seated Vairocana Buddha — span five centuries.

photo.alt: Seated Vairocana Buddha carving at the Longmen Grottoes
photo.credit: <!-- photo: longmen-grottoes-vairocana-buddha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Vairocana, Fengxian Temple, Longmen Grottoes (10240207654).jpg" by Gary Todd · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Vairocana,_Fengxian_Temple,_Longmen_Grottoes_(10240207654).jpg -->

entries.name: Mogao Caves (Dunhuang)
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed complex of Buddhist grottoes carved into a cliff near Dunhuang along the Silk Road. Hundreds of caves preserve murals and painted sculpture created over roughly a thousand years.

photo.alt: Cliff face and multi-story pavilions at the Mogao Caves
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mogao-caves-cliff.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Mogao Caves (23344276603).jpg" by Laika ac · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mogao_Caves_(23344276603).jpg -->

entries.name: Hato Caves
entries.desc: A limestone cave on the outskirts of Willemstad, formed from an ancient coral reef. Its chambers hold calcite formations, roosting bats, and ancient Arawak petroglyphs carved into the walls.

photo.alt: Stalactites and stalagmites inside Hato Caves, Curaçao
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hato-caves-interior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "HATO CAVES , CURACAO" by Jerrye and Roy Klotz MD · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:HATO_CAVES_,_CURACAO.jpg -->

entries.name: Grotte de Padirac
entries.desc: A limestone cave in the Dordogne entered via a vertical shaft, then a long boat ride along an underground river. The cavern's high vaulted ceilings and still water mirror are among the most dramatic in Europe.

photo.alt: The vertical entrance shaft of the Gouffre de Padirac, Lot, France
photo.credit: <!-- photo: padirac-gouffre-shaft.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Gouffre de Padirac 1 Luc Viatour" by Luc Viatour · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gouffre_de_Padirac_1_Luc_Viatour.jpg -->

entries.name: Lascaux Cave
entries.desc: A cave in the Dordogne famous for its Upper Paleolithic paintings of horses, bulls, and deer. The original is closed to protect the art, but a precise replica nearby recreates the painted galleries.

photo.alt: Replica cave paintings of horses at Lascaux, Dordogne, France
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lascaux-painting-replica.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Lascaux, replica 03" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lascaux,_replica_03.JPG -->

entries.name: Grotte de Font-de-Gaume
entries.desc: The only known original Paleolithic painted cave in Europe still open to the general public, in the Vézère Valley. Bison, mammoths, and reindeer rendered in ochre and manganese cover its narrow passages.

photo.alt: Entrance to the Grotte de Font-de-Gaume, Les Eyzies, Dordogne
photo.credit: <!-- photo: font-de-gaume-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Font de Gaume entrance approach" by Ethan Doyle White · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Font_de_Gaume_entrance_approach.jpg -->

entries.name: Grotte de Niaux
entries.desc: A Paleolithic cave in the French Pyrenees known for the Salon Noir, a deep chamber whose walls are covered in high-quality drawings of bison, horses, and ibex in black manganese. One of the most significant painted caves still accessible.

photo.alt: Paleolithic bison painting inside the Grotte de Niaux, Ariège, France
photo.credit: <!-- photo: niaux-bison-painting.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Niaux, bisons" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Niaux,_bisons.JPG -->

entries.name: Krubera Cave (Veryovkina)
entries.desc: Among the deepest known caves on Earth, in the Arabika Massif of the western Caucasus. Reaching its depths demands weeks-long expeditions by elite cavers and is far beyond ordinary visits.

photo.alt: Caver descending a passage inside the Krubera-Voronya cave system, Abkhazia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: krubera-cave-caver-descent.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Пещера Крубера-Воронья. Колодец Фартук" by Yuri Kasyan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%D0%9F%D0%B5%D1%89%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%B0_%D0%9A%D1%80%D1%83%D0%B1%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%B0-%D0%92%D0%BE%D1%80%D0%BE%D0%BD%D1%8C%D1%8F._%D0%9A%D0%BE%D0%BB%D0%BE%D0%B4%D0%B5%D1%86_%D0%A4%D0%B0%D1%80%D1%82%D1%83%D0%BA.jpg -->

entries.name: Aggtelek Caves (Baradla)
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed karst cave system straddling the Hungarian-Slovak border, with the Baradla cave as its centerpiece. It is known for dripstone formations and concerts held in its acoustically rich chambers.

photo.alt: Dripstone gallery inside the Baradla Cave, Aggtelek, Hungary
photo.credit: <!-- photo: baradla-cave-dripstones.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Aggtelek National Park, Baradla Cave" by Horvabe · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aggtelek_National_Park,_Baradla_Cave.jpg -->

entries.name: Vatnajökull Ice Caves
entries.desc: Seasonal caves that form within Europe's largest ice cap, their blue walls sculpted by meltwater. New caves appear each winter, so visits require a guide and take place only in the cold season.

photo.alt: Blue ice cave inside an Icelandic glacier
photo.credit: <!-- photo: iceland-glacier-ice-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Natural glacier ice cave" by sergejf · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Natural_glacier_ice_cave_(16281965008).jpg -->

entries.name: Þríhnúkagígur (Inside the Volcano)
entries.desc: A dormant volcano near Reykjavik whose drained magma chamber can be entered by a lowering lift. It is one of the few places on Earth where visitors descend into the empty heart of a volcano.

photo.alt: Visitors on a lift platform inside the drained magma chamber of Þríhnúkagígur volcano
photo.credit: <!-- photo: thrihnukagigur-volcano-crater.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Iceland thrihnukagigur-volcano inside of crater.jpg" by Uaiecs · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Iceland_thrihnukagigur-volcano_inside_of_crater.jpg -->

entries.name: Leiðarendi Lava Tube
entries.desc: A lava tube on the Reykjanes Peninsula near Reykjavik, explored on guided caving trips. Its passages show colorful mineral-stained walls, lava stalactites, and animal bones from long-trapped creatures.

photo.alt: Colorful mineral-stained lava tube ceiling with dripstone formations inside Leiðarendi cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: leidarendi-lava-tube.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Leiðarendi Lava Tube in Iceland - 2013-04-26 D.jpg" by El Coleccionista de Instantes · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lei%C3%B0arendi_Lava_Tube_in_Iceland_-_2013-04-26_D.jpg -->

entries.name: Víðgelmir Cave
entries.desc: One of the largest lava tubes on Earth, in western Iceland's Borgarfjörður region. Guided tours pass black basalt walls, stalactites of frozen lava, and striking mineral color deposits lit from below.

photo.alt: Visitors walking through the lava tube interior of Víðgelmir cave with lava drip formations overhead
photo.credit: <!-- photo: vidgelmir-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Vidgelmir-03.jpg" by Olikristinn · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Vidgelmir-03.jpg -->

entries.name: Ajanta Caves
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed set of rock-cut Buddhist monasteries and prayer halls carved into a cliff in Maharashtra. They are celebrated for ancient murals depicting the life and past lives of the Buddha.

photo.alt: Rock-cut cave facades carved into the horseshoe-shaped cliff at Ajanta
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ajanta-caves-cliff.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "A view of Ajanta Caves,Maharashtra(India).jpg" by Diksha Jadhav · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:A_view_of_Ajanta_Caves,Maharashtra(India).jpg -->

entries.name: Ellora Caves
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed complex of rock-cut temples and monasteries in Maharashtra spanning Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain faiths. Its centerpiece is the Kailasa temple, carved as a single monolith from the cliff.

photo.alt: The monolithic Kailasa temple carved from the cliff face at Ellora Caves
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ellora-kailasa-temple.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Kailasa temple overview, Ellora.jpg" by Ms Sarah Welch · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kailasa_temple_overview,_Ellora.jpg -->

entries.name: Blue Grotto (Grotta Azzurra)
entries.desc: A sea cave on the island of Capri entered by small rowboat through a low opening. Sunlight passing through an underwater cavity fills the interior with a striking, glowing blue light.

photo.alt: Glowing blue water inside the sea cave entrance of the Blue Grotto on Capri
photo.credit: <!-- photo: blue-grotto-capri.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Blue Grotto IMG 0203.JPG" by Deror_avi · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blue_Grotto_IMG_0203.JPG -->

entries.name: Grotta di Nettuno (Neptune's Grotto)
entries.desc: A sea cave on a rugged cape near Alghero, reached by boat or a steep cliff staircase called the Escala del Cabirol. Inside, a subterranean saltwater lake reflects alabaster stalactites and stalagmites.

photo.alt: Underground saltwater lake reflecting stalactites and stalagmites inside Grotta di Nettuno
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grotta-di-nettuno-lake.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "14 644 Grotta di Nettuno, Höhlensee.jpg" by Falk2 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:14_644_Grotta_di_Nettuno,_H%C3%B6hlensee.jpg -->

entries.name: Kong Lor Cave
entries.desc: A vast river cave in remote central Laos, navigated by longtail boat along an underground stretch of the Hinboun River. The passage cuts entirely through a limestone karst mountain, emerging into a valley on the other side.

photo.alt: Longtail boat entering the river cave passage of Kong Lor through the limestone karst
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kong-lor-cave-river.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Khammouane ThamKhongLor2 tango7174.jpg" by Tango7174 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Khammouane_ThamKhongLor2_tango7174.jpg -->

entries.name: Deer Cave
entries.desc: One of the world's largest cave passages, in Malaysia's UNESCO-listed Gunung Mulu National Park on Borneo. At dusk, millions of bats stream out of its mouth in a long ribbon across the sky.

photo.alt: Cave passages in Gunung Mulu National Park, Borneo
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mulu-caves-borneo.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Caves of Mulu" by Eric Lanning · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Caves_of_Mulu_(32172276551).jpg -->

entries.name: Batu Caves
entries.desc: A limestone hill north of Kuala Lumpur holding a series of caves and Hindu shrines. A tall golden statue and a long, brightly painted staircase lead up to the main temple cavern.

photo.alt: Golden Lord Murugan statue and staircase at Batu Caves
photo.credit: <!-- photo: batu-caves-murugan-statue.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Batu Caves, Lord Murugan Statue" by Shesmax · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Batu_Caves,_Lord_Murugan_Statue._2019-12-01_10-49-22.jpg -->

entries.name: Grutas de Cacahuamilpa
entries.desc: One of the largest stalactite cave systems in the world, set in Guerrero state south of Mexico City. Its guided path passes enormous chambers formed by two underground rivers, packed with formations on every surface.

photo.alt: Entrance to the Cacahuamilpa Caverns in Guerrero, Mexico
photo.credit: <!-- photo: grutas-cacahuamilpa-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Entrada grutas de cacahuamilpa - panoramio" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrada_grutas_de_cacahuamilpa_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Cenotes of Yucatán
entries.desc: Natural sinkholes formed where limestone bedrock collapses to expose groundwater, scattered across the Yucatán Peninsula. Held sacred by the Maya, many are now popular for swimming and cave diving.

photo.alt: Cenote sinkhole near Ek Balam in the Yucatán Peninsula
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cenote-ek-balam-yucatan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cenote Ek Balam, Yucatán" by Anna Bertho · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cenote_Ek_Balam,_Yucat%C3%A1n.jpg -->

entries.name: Cave of the Crystals (Cueva de los Cristales)
entries.desc: A chamber deep within the Naica mine in Chihuahua containing some of the largest natural crystals ever found. Extreme heat and humidity make it deadly to enter, and it has since been reflooded.

photo.alt: Giant selenite crystals inside the Cave of the Crystals at Naica
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cueva-de-los-cristales-naica.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Cristales cueva de Naica" by Alexander Van Driessche · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cristales_cueva_de_Naica.JPG -->

entries.name: Waitomo Glowworm Caves
entries.desc: Limestone caves on New Zealand's North Island lit by thousands of glowworms endemic to the country. Boat tours drift beneath ceilings that shimmer like a starry sky in the darkness.

photo.alt: Boat tour beneath glowing worms in the Waitomo Glowworm Grotto
photo.credit: <!-- photo: waitomo-glowworm-grotto.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Glowworm Grotto, Waitomo Glowworm Cave" by Michael Willison · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Glowworm_Grotto,_Waitomo_Glowworm_Cave.jpg -->

entries.name: Al Hoota Cave
entries.desc: A show cave at the foot of Jebel Shams in northern Oman, reached by a short electric train. It contains an underground lake home to a species of blind, translucent cave fish.

photo.alt: Entrance building at Al Hoota Cave, Oman
photo.credit: <!-- photo: al-hoota-cave-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Hoota-cave-entry" by A1000 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hoota-cave-entry.jpg -->

entries.name: Puerto Princesa Underground River
entries.desc: A navigable underground river on the island of Palawan, flowing directly into the sea. The UNESCO-listed park is toured by paddle boat through limestone chambers of stalactites and stalagmites.

photo.alt: Entrance to the Puerto Princesa Underground River, Palawan
photo.credit: <!-- photo: puerto-princesa-underground-river.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Puerto Princesa Underground River" by Mike Gonzalez · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Puerto_Princesa_Underground_River.jpg -->

entries.name: Benagil Cave
entries.desc: A sea cave on Portugal's Algarve coast famous for a circular skylight in its domed ceiling. Reached only from the water by kayak, boat, or swimming, it opens onto a small sheltered beach.

photo.alt: Aerial view into Benagil Cave's domed roof opening onto its sheltered beach on the Algarve coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: benagil-cave-algarve.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Benagil Cave seen by above.jpg" by Marian78ro · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Benagil_Cave_seen_by_above.jpg -->

entries.name: Dobšiná Ice Cave
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed ice cave in the Slovak Paradise region, holding a large volume of permanent ice. Its thick floor ice and frozen columns are viewed on short guided tours during the summer months.

photo.alt: Ice formation and floor ice inside Dobšiná Ice Cave in Slovakia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: dobsina-ice-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Dobšinská ľadová jaskyňa Ice form well.jpg" by Margoz · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Dob%C5%A1insk%C3%A1_%C4%BEadov%C3%A1_jasky%C5%88a_Ice_form_well.jpg -->

entries.name: Postojna Cave
entries.desc: A vast karst cave system in southwestern Slovenia, toured partly by electric railway. It is known for its dense limestone formations and the rare olm, a blind cave-dwelling amphibian.

photo.alt: Visitors touring a large limestone chamber inside Postojna Cave, Slovenia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: postojna-cave-interior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Postojna Cave Park (9500201121).jpg" by Michael R Perry · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Postojna_Cave_Park_(9500201121).jpg -->

entries.name: Škocjan Caves
entries.desc: A UNESCO-listed cave system carved by the Reka River, notable for one of the largest known underground canyons. Walkways cross a deep gorge lit by daylight from collapsed dolines.

photo.alt: Deep river gorge cut through limestone at Škocjan Caves, Slovenia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: skocjan-caves-canyon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Škocjan Caves - Dolima - Timavo River.jpg" by Andou · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%C5%A0kocjan_Caves_-_Dolima_-_Timavo_River.jpg -->

entries.name: Cango Caves
entries.desc: A limestone cave system in the foothills of the Swartberg mountains near Oudtshoorn. Standard tours pass large dripstone chambers, while an adventure route squeezes through narrow tunnels.

photo.alt: Large dripstone chamber with stalactite and stalagmite formations inside Cango Caves, South Africa
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cango-caves-interior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Cango Caves-001.JPG" by Rute Martins of Leoa's Photography · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cango_Caves-001.JPG -->

entries.name: Jeju Lava Tubes (Manjanggul)
entries.desc: Part of a UNESCO-listed volcanic lava tube system on Jeju Island, formed by flowing basaltic lava. A section is open to visitors, ending near one of the world's tallest known lava columns.

photo.alt: The tall basaltic lava column formation inside Manjanggul lava tube, Jeju Island
photo.credit: <!-- photo: manjanggul-lava-tube.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Manjanggul Lava Tower.jpg" by Shane Porter EssPea · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Manjanggul_Lava_Tower.jpg -->

entries.name: Drach Caves (Coves del Drac)
entries.desc: A network of limestone caves on Mallorca's east coast containing Lake Martel, one of the largest underground lakes in Europe. Visits typically include a live classical concert performed on the water.

photo.alt: Lake Martel's turquoise water beneath stalactite formations inside the Drach Caves, Mallorca
photo.credit: <!-- photo: coves-del-drac-lake-martel.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Mallorca-Porto Cristo-Coves del Drac-48E.jpg" by Rikki Mitterer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mallorca-Porto_Cristo-Coves_del_Drac-48E.jpg -->

entries.name: Nerja Caves
entries.desc: A large cave complex near the Costa del Sol, discovered in the mid-20th century. It contains one of the world's widest natural columns and prehistoric wall paintings, and hosts concerts in its main hall.

photo.alt: Lit walkway through a large stalactite-lined hall inside the Nerja Caves, Spain
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nerja-caves-interior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Cueva de Nerja 01.jpg" by Millars · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cueva_de_Nerja_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Cueva de los Verdes
entries.desc: A lava tube on Lanzarote created by an eruption of the Corona volcano thousands of years ago. Part of it was adapted for visitors and includes a concert hall and a well-known optical illusion.

photo.alt: Lava tube interior at Cueva de los Verdes, Lanzarote
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cueva-de-los-verdes-lava-tube.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Cueva de los Verdes - Lanzarote 02.jpg" by H. Zell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cueva_de_los_Verdes_-_Lanzarote_02.jpg -->

entries.name: Cueva de Altamira
entries.desc: A cave in northern Spain renowned for vivid prehistoric bison paintings that reshaped views of early art. The fragile original is largely closed, and a museum replica lets visitors see the ceiling.

photo.alt: Entrance to the Altamira cave near Santillana del Mar
photo.credit: <!-- photo: altamira-cave-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Entrada a la cueva de Altamira, Santillana del Mar.jpg" by Alonso de Mendoza · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrada_a_la_cueva_de_Altamira,_Santillana_del_Mar.jpg -->

entries.name: Dambulla Cave Temple
entries.desc: The largest and best-preserved cave temple complex in Sri Lanka, cut into a massive rock outcrop in the island's cultural triangle. Five caves contain 157 Buddha statues and murals covering roughly 2,100 square meters of ceiling and wall.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Dambulla cave temple complex
photo.credit: <!-- photo: dambulla-cave-temple-exterior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Exterior of Dambulla cave temple 07.JPG" by Cherubino · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Exterior_of_Dambulla_cave_temple_07.JPG -->

entries.name: Tham Lod Cave
entries.desc: A river cave in northern Thailand crossed by bamboo raft along a stream. It contains prehistoric wooden coffins and, at dusk, hosts a mass flight of swifts returning to roost inside.

photo.alt: Tham Lod Cave in Pang Mapha, northern Thailand
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tham-lod-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Tham Lod Cave (57638588).jpeg" by Drew Hopper · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tham_Lod_Cave_(57638588).jpeg -->

entries.name: Phraya Nakhon Cave
entries.desc: A cave in Khao Sam Roi Yot National Park reached by a steep coastal climb. A hole in its collapsed roof sends a shaft of sunlight onto a royal pavilion built there for a 19th-century king.

photo.alt: Phraya Nakhon Cave in Khao Sam Roi Yot National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: phraya-nakhon-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Phraya Nakhon Cave Prachuap Khiri Khan.jpg" by KOSIN SUKHUM · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Phraya_Nakhon_Cave_Prachuap_Khiri_Khan.jpg -->

entries.name: Cheddar Caves
entries.desc: Two show caves at the foot of Cheddar Gorge in Somerset, home to the oldest complete human skeleton found in Britain. Gough's Cave has a stalactite-filled chamber; Cox's Cave is known for colored formations.

photo.alt: Cheddar Gorge in Somerset, England
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cheddar-gorge.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Cheddar Gorge, Somerset, UK - Diliff.jpg" by Diliff · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cheddar_Gorge,_Somerset,_UK_-_Diliff.jpg -->

entries.name: Fingal's Cave
entries.desc: A sea cave on the uninhabited island of Staffa formed in hexagonal basalt columns. Its cathedral-like acoustics and geometry inspired Mendelssohn's Hebrides overture; access is by boat in season.

photo.alt: Fingal's Cave on the island of Staffa
photo.credit: <!-- photo: fingals-cave-staffa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Staffa Fingal's Cave 14712.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Staffa_Fingal%27s_Cave_14712.JPG -->

entries.name: Antelope Canyon
entries.desc: A slot canyon on Navajo land near Page, carved by flash floods into flowing sandstone walls. Midday sunbeams filtering into the narrow passages make it a landmark for photographers; entry is guided.

photo.alt: Antelope Canyon's wave-like sandstone slot canyon walls
photo.credit: <!-- photo: antelope-canyon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "USA Antelope-Canyon.jpg" by Lucas Löffler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:USA_Antelope-Canyon.jpg -->

entries.name: Carlsbad Caverns
entries.desc: A national park in the Guadalupe Mountains centered on a limestone cave with the enormous Big Room chamber. In summer, evening flights of Brazilian free-tailed bats emerge from the entrance.

photo.alt: Chinese Theater rock formations in the Big Room of Carlsbad Cavern
photo.credit: <!-- photo: carlsbad-caverns-chinese-theater.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Chinese Theater in Carlsbad Cavern-123.JPG" by Daniel Mayer · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Chinese_Theater_in_Carlsbad_Cavern-123.JPG -->

entries.name: Jewel Cave
entries.desc: A national monument in the Black Hills and one of the longest cave systems on Earth. It takes its name from the glittering calcite crystals lining many of its passage walls.

photo.alt: Entrance to Jewel Cave National Monument in the Black Hills
photo.credit: <!-- photo: jewel-cave-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Jewel Cave National Monument entrance.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Jewel_Cave_National_Monument_entrance.jpg -->

entries.name: Lechuguilla Cave
entries.desc: A deep, pristine cave in Carlsbad Caverns National Park prized by scientists for its rare gypsum and sulfur formations. Access is restricted to approved research and survey teams, not the public.

photo.alt: Gypsum chandelier formations in Lechuguilla Cave's Chandelier Ballroom
photo.credit: <!-- photo: lechuguilla-cave-chandelier-ballroom.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Lechuguilla Chandelier Ballroom.jpg" by Dave Bunnell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lechuguilla_Chandelier_Ballroom.jpg -->

entries.name: Luray Caverns
entries.desc: The largest cavern in the eastern United States, set in Virginia's Shenandoah Valley. It houses the Great Stalacpipe Organ, which produces tones by tapping calibrated stalactites across the chambers.

photo.alt: Saracen's Tent flowstone formation inside Luray Caverns
photo.credit: <!-- photo: luray-caverns-saracens-tent.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Saracen's Tent, Luray Caverns.jpg" by ZeWrestler · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Saracen%27s_Tent,_Luray_Caverns.jpg -->

entries.name: Mammoth Cave
entries.desc: The longest known cave system in the world, formed in Kentucky limestone. This UNESCO site offers ranger-led tours ranging from easy walks to strenuous routes through its extensive passages.

photo.alt: Historic Entrance to Mammoth Cave in Kentucky
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mammoth-cave-historic-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Historic Entrance (Mammoth Cave, Kentucky, USA) 28 (37096620394).jpg" by James St. John · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Historic_Entrance_(Mammoth_Cave,_Kentucky,_USA)_28_(37096620394).jpg -->

meta: Longest Cave System in the World

entries.name: Mendenhall Ice Caves
entries.desc: Meltwater-carved caves inside the Mendenhall Glacier near Juneau, reached by hiking and kayaking. The glacier is receding rapidly, so the caves shift and can become unstable from year to year.

photo.alt: Ice cave formed under the Mendenhall Glacier near Juneau
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mendenhall-ice-cave.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "An ice cave under Mendenhall Glacier.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:An_ice_cave_under_Mendenhall_Glacier.jpg -->

entries.name: Sea Lion Caves
entries.desc: A large sea cave on the Oregon coast that serves as a wintering haul-out for Steller sea lions. Visitors descend by elevator to a viewing area above the cavern and its resident colony.

photo.alt: Interior of Sea Lion Caves on the Oregon coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sea-lion-caves-interior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Inside the Cave.JPG" by Oregongirlmary · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Inside_the_Cave.JPG -->

entries.name: Wind Cave
entries.desc: A national park cave in the Black Hills, among the longest and most complex in the world. It is renowned for boxwork, a rare honeycomb-like calcite formation found here in unusual abundance.

photo.alt: Calcite boxwork formation inside Wind Cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: wind-cave-boxwork.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Calcite boxwork, Wind Cave.jpg" by James St. John · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Calcite_boxwork,_Wind_Cave.jpg -->

entries.name: Hang Én
entries.desc: One of the largest caves in the world, in central Vietnam's Phong Nha-Kẻ Bàng park. Trekkers reach it on foot through jungle and rivers and camp overnight on a beach beneath its huge arch.

photo.alt: Huge cave arch entrance at Hang Én, Phong Nha-Kẻ Bàng National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hang-en-cave-arch.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Hang Én Cave - 201505 - JB.jpg" by Jérémie B. · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hang_%C3%89n_Cave_-_201505_-_JB.jpg -->

entries.name: Phong Nha Cave
entries.desc: A river cave in the UNESCO-listed Phong Nha-Kẻ Bàng National Park, entered by boat along an underground stream. It is one of the region's most accessible showcaves amid a landscape of limestone karst.

photo.alt: Boats entering the river-cave mouth of Phong Nha Cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: phong-nha-cave-river-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Vào động Phong Nha.jpg" by Bùi Thụy Đào Nguyên · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:V%C3%A0o_%C4%91%E1%BB%99ng_Phong_Nha.jpg -->

entries.name: Sơn Đoòng Cave
entries.desc: Widely regarded as the largest cave passage in the world, in central Vietnam's Phong Nha-Kẻ Bàng park. Collapsed ceilings create skylit jungles inside, reached only on a permitted multi-day expedition.

photo.alt: Skylit jungle doline inside Sơn Đoòng Cave
photo.credit: <!-- photo: son-doong-cave-doline.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Son Doong Cave DB (3).jpg" by Dave Bunnell · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Son_Doong_Cave_DB_(3).jpg -->

entries.name: Sung Sot Cave (Surprise Cave)
entries.desc: One of the largest and most visited caves in UNESCO-listed Ha Long Bay, set on Bo Hon Island. A lit walkway winds through two vast chambers of stalactites reached by boat and a short climb.

photo.alt: Illuminated stalactite chamber inside Sung Sot Cave, Ha Long Bay
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sung-sot-cave-interior.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Hang Sửng Sốt 3.jpg" by Mike Colvin from Manchester, UK · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Hang_S%E1%BB%ADng_S%E1%BB%91t_3.jpg -->
